sanskrit:
मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु।
मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः॥
transliteration:
man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru |
mām evaiṣyasi yuktvāivam ātmānaṁ mat-parāyaṇaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Fix mind on Me, be My devotee, My worshipper, bow to Me; thus uniting yourself and making Me your supreme goal, you will come to Me alone.
contextual_meaning: Krishna prescribes a single-pointed relational therapy: exclusive attunement to an internalized benevolent presence dissolves fragmented identities and delivers the psyche to a state of secure wholeness.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to an internalized representation of unconditional acceptance; ego boundaries soften into participatory identity without loss of agency.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out caregiver who can’t stop ruminating about work adopts a nightly 10-minute ritual of mentally placing herself in the presence of a compassionate figure, gradually feeling the intrusive thoughts lose their grip.
anchor_text: Center your mind and heart on Me alone; let every thought and act flow toward Me.
